description: Standalone Cusco tours separated from the main catalog. The published price is calculated so the estimated final net amount after PayPal and bank withdrawal equals twice the internal tour cost. Extras are not doubled; they only cover payment and withdrawal fees.

exchangeRate.notes: Use 3.75 as the operating exchange rate until an automatic source is integrated. The exchange rate should not remain hardcoded inside the JS or each tour.

tourMarkup.description: The agency should receive a net amount in the bank account equal to twice the internal tour cost.

extrasMarkup.description: Extras, tickets and meals are not doubled. Only the amount needed to cover fees and receive the indicated net cost is published.

formula.rounding: round up to commercial USD ending .90

products.title: Ancestral Welcome to Cusco
products.shortDescription: Enjoy a scenic introduction to Cusco with an Andean welcome ceremony, textile center visit and panoramic views from Cristo Blanco.
products.description: Enjoy a scenic introduction to Cusco with an Andean welcome ceremony, textile center visit and panoramic views from Cristo Blanco.

itinerary.title: Meeting and departure by panoramic bus
itinerary.description: We will meet at Plaza Regocijo or the previously coordinated point. From there, we will begin the route on a double-decker panoramic bus, ideal for a first visual connection with the city of Cusco and its surroundings.

itinerary.title: Scenic route through Cusco
itinerary.description: During the ride, the guide will share historical and cultural insights while you enjoy representative areas of the city, views toward Sacsayhuaman and urban landscapes ideal for photos.

itinerary.title: Andean ceremony de bienvenida
itinerary.description: You will take part in a symbolic Mother Earth offering ceremony guided by an Andean priest. This experience is designed as a ritual of harmony, gratitude and good energy to begin your journey through Cusco and Machu Picchu.

itinerary.title: Textile center and Andean culture
itinerary.description: We will visit a textile center specializing in alpaca and baby alpaca fiber, where you will learn about traditional weaving techniques, natural dyes and details of Andean clothing.

itinerary.title: Cristo Blanco viewpoint
itinerary.description: We will finish at the Cristo Blanco viewpoint, one of Cusco’s most photographed scenic spots, with time to enjoy the city views and capture memorable images.

faq.q: How long does the experience last?
faq.a: The reference duration is 2 h 30 min. The final schedule is coordinated before departure so you clearly know the pickup, visits and return time.

faq.q: Does the tour include a guide?
faq.a: Yes. All our tours include a professional guide in Spanish and English. Other languages are available upon request/coordination and may have an additional cost.

faq.q: Which tickets or extras should I consider?
faq.a: If a special entrance ticket applies for your date, the reservations team will review it with you before payment.

faq.q: Where does the service start?
faq.a: Pickup or the meeting point is coordinated according to the tour, your hotel and the operation of the day. We send the final details by WhatsApp or email before the experience.

faq.q: What should I bring?
faq.a: We recommend bringing your passport or ID, water, sun protection, comfortable clothes, a light jacket and cash for personal expenses or tickets not included.

paymentOptions.partialPaymentLabel: Reserve with USD 10.00 per person and pay the balance in Cusco

products.title: Cusco City Tour + Archaeological Sites
products.shortDescription: Visit Cusco’s most important nearby archaeological sites, including Sacsayhuamán, Qenqo, Puka Pukara and Tambomachay.

itinerary.title: Historic center of Cusco
itinerary.description: We will begin the route with an introduction to the imperial city, its colonial streets, temples and historic spaces that reveal the fusion of Inca and Spanish architecture.

itinerary.title: Sacsayhuamán
itinerary.description: We will visit the impressive archaeological complex of Sacsayhuaman, known for its enormous perfectly fitted stone blocks and its ceremonial importance for ancient Cusco.

itinerary.title: Qenqo, Puka Pukara and Tambomachay
itinerary.description: We will continue to the archaeological sites near the city. You will discover ceremonial spaces, ancient control points and structures linked to water worship.

itinerary.title: Pisac archaeological site
itinerary.description: Departure from Cusco to the Sacred Valley to visit the Pisac archaeological site, famous for its agricultural terraces, panoramic views and Inca heritage.

itinerary.title: Pisac craft market
itinerary.description: Time to explore the craft market and discover local products, textiles, ceramics and pieces inspired by Andean iconography.

itinerary.title: Urubamba
itinerary.description: We will arrive in Urubamba, the heart of the Sacred Valley, where you will have time for an optional buffet lunch before continuing the route.

itinerary.title: Ollantaytambo
itinerary.description: We will visit the Ollantaytambo archaeological site, an ancient living Inca town and a strategic point in the valley because of its architecture, terraces and temples.

itinerary.title: Chinchero
itinerary.description: We will close the route with a cultural visit in Chinchero, known for its textile traditions, Andean landscape and historic heritage.

products.title: South Valley of Cusco
products.shortDescription: Explore Tipon, Pikillacta and Andahuaylillas on a cultural half-day route south of Cusco.
products.description: This half-day tour starts with pickup at your Cusco hotel between 8:00 and 8:30 AM. We travel south to visit Tipon, known for its Inca hydraulic engineering; Pikillacta, an ancient Wari citadel; and Andahuaylillas Church, famous for its murals and baroque altarpieces. Return to Cusco is usually around 2:30 PM.
includes: Professional guide in Spanish and English

itinerary.title: Departure from Cusco
itinerary.description: Pickup from your hotel between 8:00 and 8:30 AM to begin the route to southern Cusco.

itinerary.title: Tipon
itinerary.description: Visit the Tipon archaeological site, famous for its Inca hydraulic system and still-functioning water channels.

itinerary.title: Pikillacta
itinerary.description: Explore the ancient Wari citadel of Pikillacta, an important example of pre-Inca urban planning.

itinerary.title: Andahuaylillas
itinerary.description: Visit Andahuaylillas Church, known as the Sistine Chapel of the Americas for its murals, canvases and baroque altarpieces.

itinerary.title: Pickup and departure
itinerary.description: Pickup between 4:30 and 5:00 AM from your hotel in Cusco to begin the trip to the south of the region.

itinerary.title: Breakfast en Cusipata
itinerary.description: Stop in Cusipata for breakfast before continuing to the community of Palcoyo.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Palcoyo
itinerary.description: Arrival in the Palcoyo area, where colorful mountains and high-Andean landscapes can be seen.

itinerary.title: Hike and stone forest
itinerary.description: Accessible hike through the colorful mountain range and stone forest.

itinerary.title: Lunch and return
itinerary.description: Lunch en route and return to Cusco during the afternoon.

faq.q: How long does the experience last?
faq.a: The reference duration is Full Day. The final schedule is coordinated before departure so you clearly know the pickup, visits and return time.

faq.q: Does the tour include a guide?
faq.a: Yes. All our tours include a professional guide in Spanish and English. Other languages are available upon request/coordination and may have an additional cost.

faq.q: Which tickets or extras should I consider?
faq.a: Entrance tickets, tourist tickets, lunches or other extras are shown in the extras section so you can add or review them before booking.

faq.q: Where does the service start?
faq.a: Pickup or the meeting point is coordinated according to the tour, your hotel and the operation of the day. We send the final details by WhatsApp or email before the experience.

faq.q: What should I bring?
faq.a: We recommend bringing your passport or ID, water, sun protection, comfortable clothes, a light jacket and cash for personal expenses or tickets not included.

paymentOptions.partialPaymentLabel: Reserve with USD 14.90 per person and pay the balance in Cusco

internalPricing.pricingRule: published price must produce targetNetPEN after PayPal and bank withdrawal fees

pricingNotes: The published base price is calculated so the net amount received equals twice the internal tour cost.
Extras are not doubled; they are calculated to recover the indicated cost after fees.
Update the operating exchange rate from currency-config.json before publishing final prices.

seo.title: Palcoyo Rainbow Mountain | My Cusco Trip
seo.description: Explore Palcoyo’s colorful mountain range and stone forest on a more accessible alternative to Vinicunca.

products.title: Seven Lakes of Ausangate
products.shortDescription: Discover the Seven Lakes of Ausangate on a full-day hike surrounded by glaciers, high-Andean landscapes and impressive mountain scenery.
products.description: Discover the Seven Lakes of Ausangate on a full-day hike surrounded by glaciers, high-Andean landscapes and impressive mountain scenery.
includes: Professional guide in Spanish and English
excludes: Entrance to the lagoons if a local payment applies
Pacchanta hot springs if an additional payment applies

itinerary.title: Pickup and departure from Cusco
itinerary.description: Pickup between 4:00 and 4:30 AM from your hotel and trip to Pacchanta, in the Ausangate area.

itinerary.title: Breakfast en Pacchanta
itinerary.description: Arrival in Pacchanta, breakfast and guide explanation about the route, altitude and hiking recommendations.

itinerary.title: Start of the hike
itinerary.description: Start of the trek along high-Andean trails with views of the Ausangate snow-capped mountain and the Vilcanota range.

itinerary.title: Route through the 7 lagoons
itinerary.description: Visit the lagoons on the circuit, known for their turquoise, emerald green and deep blue tones.

itinerary.title: Return to Pacchanta
itinerary.description: Descent to Pacchanta. Depending on the day’s operation, there may be an option to enjoy the area’s hot springs.

itinerary.title: Lunch and regreso a Cusco
itinerary.description: Lunch in the area and return by tourist transport to Cusco, arriving at sunset.
